commit.message:
Fix spurious autosave on artifact open caused by MDXEditor normalization

MDXEditor fires onChange on mount with normalized markdown, triggering
autosave without any user edit. Now tracks dirty state and suppresses
the first body onChange event after load.

commit.message:
Add playlist size cap and track protection to rotation schedules (#139)

* Add playlist size cap and protect top N to rotation

Rotation schedules now support two optional parameters:
- target_size: playlist size cap with ±5 tolerance; when exceeded,
  extra tracks are archived to bring the playlist back in range
- protect_count: position-based protection for the first N tracks
  in the playlist, keeping them exempt from rotation

Both params work across all three rotation modes (archive_oldest,
refresh, swap). The workshop UI exposes these as optional fields
in the rotation configuration form.

Adds 15 new tests covering _compute_rotation_count helper,
protect_count integration, and target_size overflow behavior.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CW3xET19AKPCiiZAqfFckB

* Replace ±5 tolerance with hard playlist size cap for rotation

The target_size parameter now enforces a strict cap — rotation will
archive enough tracks to bring the playlist to or under the target,
rather than allowing a 5-track buffer before triggering extra archival.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CW3xET19AKPCiiZAqfFckB

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add mobile optimizations + HLS adaptive bitrate streaming

Mobile optimizations:
- Micro-segment turbo start (first 1.5s blob-preloaded for instant play)
- 720p mobile rendition (UA-based serving, 40-60% smaller files)
- Canvas poster bridge (zero-gap poster-to-video transition)
- 103 Early Hints (Link preload headers for CDN HTTP 103)
- VMAF-targeted encoding (binary search for optimal CRF per content)

HLS adaptive streaming:
- Three quality renditions (1080p stream-copy, 720p CRF 24, 480p CRF 26)
- fMP4/CMAF segments with 6s segment duration
- Separate hls-package BullMQ queue (auto-enqueued after render)
- useHls hook (Safari native, Chrome/Firefox hls.js, MP4 fallback)
- Integrated in both SimpleMobilePlayer and SmartSyncPlayer
- Project goes ready with MP4 immediately; HLS upgrades async

commit.message:
feat(#3): add 021f/5100 research handlers and improve 0307 session-count logging (#17)

OCLEANY3P (Oclean X Pro Elite, sw=1.0.0.41) responds to CMD_QUERY_RUNNING_DATA_T1
(0307) with two previously unhandled notification types:

  021f – candidate: zone/area pressure data (analogous to 2604 on OCLEANY3M)
  5100 – candidate: session metadata with implicit year (analogous to 5a00)

Both handlers log all bytes for protocol research. The byte layout cannot be
confirmed without correlated post-sync brushing data.

Also improves 0307 parser logging: when year_byte=0 (no inline session), the
log now includes the session_count so it's visible that stored sessions exist.

Sources: C5733b.java (multi-packet reassembly), C3376s.java (Type-1 handler)
in the decompiled Oclean APK.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(orchestrator): make summarisation _arun non-blocking via ainvoke (#70)

* fix(orchestrator): make summarisation _arun non-blocking via ainvoke

Replaces the sync `self._run()` delegation in `_arun` with a direct
`await llm.ainvoke(prompt)` call, preventing event-loop blocking on
every async summarisation invocation.

- Extract shared prompt construction into `_build_prompt` helper
- Use `extract_text` from `backend.llm` for consistent response
  normalisation in both `_run` and `_arun`
- Add 7 unit tests covering sync and async paths, including a guard
  that verifies `_run` is never called from `_arun`

Closes #28

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(summarisation): address PR review comments

- Remove unused `import asyncio`
- Narrow broad `except Exception` to `except ImportError` to avoid masking real regressions
- Add `test_run_respects_max_tokens` to sync test class (parity with async)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

CRITICAL FIX: Add missing src/data files to repository

THE ROOT CAUSE: src/data folder was gitignored!
- The data/ folder was in .gitignore, which excluded src/data
- This is why Vercel couldn't find theoryContent.ts

FIXES APPLIED:
1. Updated .gitignore to only ignore /data/ (root data folder)
2. Added src/data/*.ts files to the repository
3. Created theoryContentExports.ts as additional safeguard
4. Updated Explanations.tsx to use theoryContentExports
5. Bumped version to 1.0.2 for cache clear

Files now included:
- src/data/theoryContent.ts
- src/data/theoryContentExports.ts
- src/data/theoryLaws.ts

This definitively fixes the Vercel build error.

commit.message:
Fix _is_claude_alive false positive after Claude CLI exits

The function was checking 80 lines of scrollback for "Claude Code",
which persists in the startup banner after Claude exits. This caused
it to send /usage to a bare shell, getting no response → permanent
error state. Now checks only last 15 lines for ❯ (Claude's TUI prompt).

commit.message:
initial commit: ethkit — pure TypeScript ethers.js replacement

Minimal, function-based Ethereum library with zero native dependencies.
Only depends on @noble/hashes and @noble/curves.

Covers: ABI encode/decode, wallet signing (EIP-1559 + legacy), transaction
serialization, provider (HTTP/JSON-RPC), Interface, Contract, units,
hashing (keccak256), address (EIP-55), and common utilities.

159 comparison tests verify output matches ethers.js exactly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: ethkit
repository.description: Pure TypeScript Ethereum library. Lightweight, function-based drop-in replacement for ethers.js.

commit.message:
fix: total = slot price x convives, not sum of item prices

Set unit_price: 0 on order_lines (items are part of a fixed-price
menu). The menu_unit_price field carries the per-convive per-slot
price. This prevents any downstream SUM(unit_price) from inflating
the total by the number of categories (4x).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: multi-source candidate search via Serper.dev

Migrate from Google CSE (sunsetting Jan 2027) to Serper.dev for candidate
sourcing. Search now supports 7 sources: LinkedIn, Indeed, GitHub, Stack
Overflow, Glassdoor, Behance, Dribbble — executed in parallel server-side.

- Add candidateSearch onCall cloud function with parallel Serper execution
- Add source-specific result parsers (LinkedIn, Indeed, GitHub, SO, etc.)
- Add Firestore-based search cache with per-source TTL (24-72h)
- Add SourceSelector, SourceBadge, SourceTabs frontend components
- Replace browser-side CSE API calls with server-side cloud function
- Add source column to CSV export
- Fix pre-existing missing Clarvida stubs (ExtractionProgressPanel, etc.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add weekly pulse rhythm model with 7 predictable demand waves

Implement the daily pulse pattern for Paris VTC demand:
1. Morning Station + Airport (05:30-09:00)
2. Morning Office Absorption (07:30-10:00)
3. Midday Redistribution (11:30-14:30)
4. Evening Office Release (17:30-20:30)
5. Event Exit Release (20:30-23:45)
6. Late Station/Airport Forced Mobility (21:30-01:30)
7. Nightlife Closure Cascade (00:30-05:30)

Each wave has day-specific modifiers (e.g., nightlife peaks Sat, office dead weekends).

New files:
- weekly-pulse.ts: Core model with wave definitions, weekly rhythm
- weekly-pulse-builder.ts: Bridge to FlowState API contract

Adds WeeklyPulse to FlowState depth payload.

commit.message:
Fix 28 bugs, add 3 TTS engines and voice designer to replace ElevenLabs

Bug fixes (28 across 20 files):
- Fix Kokoro voice blending: was parsed but ignored, now uses tensor mixing
- Fix dynamic_voice weights: decimal format vs integer percentage parser
- Fix French numbers 70-99 (TENS_FR wrong values)
- Fix text segments dropped in timing_humanizer split loop
- Fix emotion intensity string comparison (now numeric via _INTENSITY_ORDER)
- Fix bio_acoustics pink noise DC offset
- Fix 6 bare except: clauses (podcast_server, tts_engine, french_preprocessor,
  markdown_parser x2, tts_hq_engine)
- Fix substring matching → word boundaries (narrative_context, emotion_analyzer,
  llm_emotion_detector)
- Fix llm_enhancer JSON parsing (5 fragile split occurrences → regex)
- Fix audio_crossfade empty curve dtype + add micro-fades for short segments
- Fix tts_hybrid_engine missing resample for Kokoro dialogue audio
- Fix synthesis_cache generation_time measuring wrong operation
- Fix conversation_generator IndexError on empty voices dict
- Fix tts_unified asyncio deadlock + int16 format assumption
- Fix voice_morphing soft clipping threshold + output clipping

New engines:
- Chatterbox (Resemble AI): beats ElevenLabs 63.75%, voice cloning, 23 languages
- Orpheus (Canopy Labs): native emotion tags, Llama-3B backbone, 8 voices
- Parler TTS (HuggingFace): description-based voice control, multilingual French

New features:
- Voice Designer: custom voice creation by tensor interpolation (9 presets)
- Spectral noise gate + noisereduce integration in audio enhancer
- NativeAudioEnhancer: full broadcast mastering without ffmpeg
- CLI: --engine parler, --voice-preset, --list-presets

Tests: 450 → 548 (+98 new tests, 0 failures)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix crash when saving project editor changes

The editor window was being deallocated while still closing because
currentEditorWindow was set to nil in the onSave callback before
window?.close() completed. Now cleanup happens in windowWillClose
via onDismiss callback, ensuring the window fully closes first.

Fix Stripe payment: remove elements.submit() for clientSecret mode

elements.submit() is only for deferred intent mode. When using clientSecret
mode (from Medusa payment session), call stripe.confirmPayment() directly.
This was causing "An unexpected error occurred" on payment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: use OpenAI Conversations API for multi-turn benchmark flow

Instead of re-sending the full conversation history as a template variable
(historytext) on every benchmark turn, we now chain responses using the
OpenAI Conversations API (previous_response_id).

How it works:
- store: true is sent on every OpenAI call so responses are persisted
- The response ID is returned as responseId in LLMCallOutput / BenchmarkTurnOutput
- On turn 2+, previousResponseId is passed back so OpenAI maintains the
  conversation context server-side
- When previousResponseId is set, historytext is sent as empty to avoid
  duplicating history that's already in the chained context
- Anthropic mode is unaffected — conversationHistory continues to work as before

Benefits:
- Eliminates growing historytext payloads each turn (better for long sessions)
- Reasoning models preserve their reasoning items across turns (better quality)
- Cleaner separation of current-turn context vs conversation history

Changes:
- lib/llm/types.ts: add previousResponseId to LLMCallInput, responseId to LLMCallOutput
- lib/benchmark/types.ts: add previousResponseId/responseId to BenchmarkTurnInput/Output
- lib/llm/OpenAILLMClient.ts: pass store+previous_response_id, return response.id
- lib/benchmark/services/SingleLlmBenchmarkConversationService.ts: thread responseId
- app/page.tsx: store openAiResponseIdRef, pass/update across turns, reset on new session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
